Package de.uka.ilkd.key.java.expression.literal

Class IntLiteral

java.lang.Object

de.uka.ilkd.key.java.expression.Literal

de.uka.ilkd.key.java.expression.literal.IntLiteral
All Implemented Interfaces:
Expression, ModelElement, ProgramElement, SourceElement, TerminalProgramElement, SVSubstitute
public class IntLiteral extends AbstractIntegerLiteral
Int literal.

  • Constructor Details

    • IntLiteral

      public IntLiteral(int value)
      Creates a new IntLiteral representing the given int.
      Parameters:
      value - the int value represented by the literal

    • IntLiteral

      public IntLiteral(String valStr)
      Creates a new IntLiteral from the given String. The String is parsed and checked for range. The input may be any String containing a literal as described by the Java 8 Language Specification. This includes hexadecimal, decimal, octal, and binary literals as well as literals containing underscores as separators. In addition, a preceding '-' sign is allowed.
      Parameters:
      valStr - the String that contains the literal
      Throws:
      NumberFormatException - if the given String does not represent a syntactically valid literal or represents a value out of int range
      See Also:
      • http://docs.oracle.com/javase/specs/jls/se8/html/jls-3.html#jls-3.10.1

    • IntLiteral

      public IntLiteral(ExtList children, String valStr)
      Constructor for Recoder2KeY transformation.
      Parameters:
      children - the children of this AST element as KeY classes, may contain: Comments
      valStr - the value of the literal
      Throws:
      NumberFormatException - if the given String does not represent a syntactically valid literal or represents a value out of int range

    • getValue

      public long getValue()

      getValue in class AbstractIntegerLiteral

    • getValueString

      public String getValueString()
      Specified by:
      getValueString in class AbstractIntegerLiteral
      Returns:
      the actual value of the literal converted to a decimal String. If the literal represents a negative value, the first character is a '-' sign.

    • parseFromString

      protected int parseFromString(String sourceStr) throws NumberFormatException
      Parses the String and extracts the actual value of the literal. This method is able to parse literals as described in the Java 8 Language Specification: hexadecimal (beginning with '0x'), decimal, octal (beginning with '0'), and binary (beginning with '0b') literals. In addition, underscores are allowed as separators inside the literal. All values parsed by this method are checked for range correctly, particularly considering the asymmetric range of int. Hexadecimal, octal and binary literals are converted using two's complement.
      Parameters:
      sourceStr - the String containing the value
      Returns:
      the parsed value as a long
      Throws:
      NumberFormatException - if the given String does not represent a syntactically valid literal or represents a value out of int range
      See Also:
      • http://docs.oracle.com/javase/specs/jls/se8/html/jls-3.html#jls-3.10.1
